data-wrapper AI Reference
=========================

data-wrapper is a zero-dependency, HTML-first reactivity library built around
one custom element: <data-wrapper>. It loads ordinary HTML view files and binds
DOM effects to ES module exports with a small attribute syntax.

There is no JSX, virtual DOM, compiler, text interpolation, signal API, or
component class API. The framework syntax is HTML attributes plus normal ES
modules.


1. Install and Mount
--------------------

Load the browser artifact and mount a view:

```html
<script src="https://unpkg.com/data-wrapper/dist/data-wrapper.min.js"></script>
<data-wrapper src="/views/counter.html"></data-wrapper>
```

When a component module imports from "data-wrapper", the host page needs an
import map:

```html
<script type="importmap">
{
    "imports": {
        "data-wrapper": "https://unpkg.com/data-wrapper/dist/data-wrapper.js"
    }
}
</script>
```


2. View Files
-------------

A view is an HTML file. It may contain one module script:

```html
<script type="module" data-module="@view/counter">
    export let count = 0;
    export function increment() {
        count += 1;
    }
</script>

<button @click="increment">+</button>
<output $text="count"></output>
```

The module script is removed before the view body is inserted. The module is
registered through an import map using its `data-module` name.

Rules:

- A view may contain zero or one `script[type="module"][data-module]`.
- A component module script must have a `data-module` name.
- A `data-module` name may be reused only by the same canonical view URL.
- A default export, when present, must be a factory function.
- A factory may return an object of per-wrapper instance bindings or nothing.
- Instance bindings shadow named module exports with the same name.


3. The Three Tokens
-------------------

The template syntax has exactly three token prefixes:

- `$`: read a binding and write to a DOM property or attribute.
- `*`: read a binding and drive a structural directive.
- `@`: listen for a DOM event and invoke or emit an action.

Examples:

```html
<output $text="count"></output>
<img $src="avatarUrl">
<button @click="save?prevent">Save</button>

<template *list="todos">
    <li $text="./task"></li>
</template>

<template *if="todos?count">
    <p>There are todos.</p>
</template>

<template *src="panel">
    <p>Choose a panel.</p>
</template>
```

Do not invent other binding syntaxes. There is no `{{ value }}` interpolation,
no `*for`, no `*each`, no `*match`, no `bind:value`, and no inline JavaScript
expression syntax.


4. `$` Property Bindings
------------------------

`$prop="binding"` reads a binding and writes it to a DOM property if that
property exists. Otherwise it writes an attribute.

Common aliases:

- `$text` writes `textContent`.
- `$class` appends a class string to the element's existing class list.
- `$unsafeHTML` writes `innerHTML` and is an explicit raw-HTML opt-in.

Blocked sinks:

- `$innerHTML` throws. Use `$text` or `$unsafeHTML`.
- `$outerHTML` throws. Use `$text` or `$unsafeHTML`.
- `$onclick` and other `$on*` event-handler sinks throw. Use `@click`.

Nullish text clears to an empty string. URL-shaped bound attributes pass a
scheme allowlist before being written.


5. `@` Event Bindings
---------------------

`@event="action"` listens for a browser event on the element. The assignment is
a data-wrapper path, not inline JavaScript.

```html
<form @submit="addTodo?prevent">
    <input name="task">
    <button>Add</button>
</form>
```

Supported event modifiers are query params:

- `?prevent` calls `event.preventDefault()`.
- `?stop` calls `event.stopPropagation()`.
- `?immediate` calls `event.stopImmediatePropagation()`.

If the path names an exported function, the component runtime invokes that
function when the event fires. If there is no matching function, the binding
emits a bubbling CustomEvent with the assigned path as the event name. This is
useful for child-to-parent signaling.

The CustomEvent detail contains:

- `originalEvent`: the original DOM event.
- `path`: the assigned action path.
- `isRel`: whether the path was relative.
- `item`: the nearest `*list` row item, when inside a row.


6. `*` Directives
-----------------

There are exactly three built-in directives:

- `*list`
- `*if`
- `*src`

Directives live on `<template>` elements. Structural template bodies should use
one root element because the runtime clones the first element child.

`*list`

`*list` reads an array and clones the template body once per item. Each row gets
a row binding context. The default row key is `id`; override it with `?key=...`.

```html
<ul>
    <template *list="todos?key=id">
        <li>
            <input type="checkbox" $checked="./done" @change="toggleTodo">
            <span $text="./task"></span>
        </li>
    </template>
</ul>
```

`*if`

`*if` inserts the template body when the bound value is truthy and removes it
when the value is falsy.

```html
<template *if="todos?count">
    <p>You have todos.</p>
</template>
```

`*src`

`*src` is a composition outlet. It fills the outlet from one of three sources:

- a non-empty string: creates a child `<data-wrapper src="...">`;
- a ChildNode or array of ChildNodes: projects captured children;
- otherwise: renders the template body as fallback.

```html
<template *src="panel">
    <p>Choose a panel.</p>
</template>
```


7. Binding Path Grammar
-----------------------

Bindings use DWRL path syntax. The parser is exported as `p` and `pURL`.

- `name` reads the nearest row that owns `name`, then parent rows, then the
  component instance or module.
- `name/path` reads nested data through slash paths.
- `./name` reads from the nearest row only.
- `../name` reads from the parent row. Repeat `../` for higher row scopes.
- `/name` reads from the component root scope and bypasses row scopes.
- `//wrapperId/name` reads the root scope of another loaded
  `<data-wrapper id="wrapperId">`.
- `?formatter` and `?formatter=value` apply formatters on `$` and `*`.
- `?prevent`, `?stop`, and `?immediate` modify `@` event handling.

Protocol-prefixed forms such as `localStorage://key` are reserved for future
source resolvers. They are inert today.

Examples:

```html
<span $text="title"></span>
<span $text="./task"></span>
<span $text="../project/name"></span>
<span $text="/title"></span>
<span $text="//orders/orderCount"></span>
<span $text="name?trim&case=title"></span>
```


8. Module and Factory Contract
------------------------------

Named exports are the shared module state surface. If a view is mounted more
than once, named exports are shared by every wrapper using that module.

Use a default factory for per-wrapper state:

```html
<script type="module" data-module="@view/counter">
    import { action } from 'data-wrapper';

    export const label = 'Counter';

    export default ({ props, cleanup }) => {
        let count = Number(props.start ?? 0);
        const timer = setInterval(() => {}, 1000);
        cleanup(() => clearInterval(timer));

        return {
            get count() { return count; },
            increment: action(() => { count += 1; }),
        };
    };
</script>

<h2 $text="label"></h2>
<button @click="increment">+</button>
<output $text="count"></output>
```

The factory receives:

- `wrapper`: the `<data-wrapper>` element.
- `url`: a URL object for the loaded view.
- `params`: `url.searchParams`.
- `props`: resolved query-param inputs plus `props.url`.
- `slots`: captured light-DOM child groups keyed by `slot`.
- `cleanup(off)`: registers teardown callbacks.

Query params on `src` become props. Assigned literal values are parsed with
`JSON.parse` when possible:

```html
<data-wrapper src="/views/counter.html?start=5&debug=true"></data-wrapper>
```

If a query value resolves to a parent binding, the prop is a stable reader
function. If it does not resolve, it becomes a static value.

The original full `src` string is always available as `props.url`.


9. Reactivity
-------------

data-wrapper does not track individual property reads. Each component runtime
tracks active DOM sinks and re-reads them during a flush. Changed outputs are
published with `Object.is` comparison.

Use `action()` for functions that mutate state:

```js
import { action } from 'data-wrapper';

export let count = 0;
export const increment = action(() => {
    count += 1;
});
```

When a wrapped action returns, a microtask flush is scheduled. If the action
returns a Promise, another flush runs when the Promise settles.

Use `flush()` when mutation happens outside an action:

```js
import { flush } from 'data-wrapper';

setTimeout(() => {
    count += 1;
    flush();
}, 1000);
```

When a binding reads a function as a value, the function is called with no
arguments. This is how computed exports work:

```js
export const doubled = () => count * 2;
```

Then:

```html
<output $text="doubled"></output>
```


10. Formatters
--------------

Formatters run left-to-right from query params on `$` bindings and `*`
directive sources. Formatter arguments are static strings.

Built-in formatters:

- `default`
- `bool`
- `case`
- `trim`
- `truncate`
- `count`
- `join`
- `sort`
- `unique`
- `number`
- `fixed`
- `percent`
- `currency`
- `date`
- `time`
- `datetime`
- `json`

Examples:

```html
<span $text="name?trim&case=title"></span>
<span $text="price?currency=EUR"></span>
<span $text="done?bool=done:active"></span>
<template *list="orders?unique=id&sort=-createdAt"></template>
```

Register custom formatters through `DW_FORMATTERS`:

```js
import { DW_FORMATTERS } from 'data-wrapper';

DW_FORMATTERS.set('initials', value => {
    return String(value ?? '')
        .trim()
        .split(/\s+/)
        .map(part => part[0] ?? '')
        .join('')
        .toUpperCase();
});
```


11. Public Exports
------------------

Public runtime exports:

- `action`
- `flush`
- `DW_DIRECTIVES`
- `DW_FORMATTERS`
- `nearestItem`
- `emit`
- `on`
- `p`
- `pURL`
- `q`

Public types include component context/factory/module types, directive types,
formatter type, item type, dispatch detail type, and `Off`.


12. Security Contract
---------------------

Loaded views are trusted code. A view loaded through `src` or through a string
value passed to `*src` may execute its module script with full page privileges.

Rules:

- Never flow user input into `<data-wrapper src="...">`.
- Never flow user input into a string used by `*src`.
- Do not serve user uploads from the same origin as the app.
- Use `$text` for untrusted text.
- Use `$unsafeHTML` only for trusted or sanitized HTML.
- `$srcdoc` is an explicit raw-HTML sink.

Same-origin loading:

- `load()` resolves `src` against `document.baseURI`.
- It trusts only `document.location.origin`.
- Cross-origin view URLs are blocked before fetch.

Bound URL allowlist:

- Schemeless values pass.
- `http:` and `https:` pass for URL-shaped attributes.
- `mailto:` and `tel:` additionally pass on `href`.
- `data:` and `blob:` additionally pass on `src` and `poster`.
- Unknown schemes are dropped with a console warning.

The es-module-shims fallback can be configured with `data-shim-src`. If
`data-shim-integrity` is present on the same script tag, the injected shim
script receives `integrity` and `crossOrigin = 'anonymous'`.


13. Error Behavior
------------------

The runtime throws for:

- unknown directives such as `*for`;
- `$innerHTML`, `$outerHTML`, and `$on*` property bindings;
- failed view fetches, with status attribution;
- more than one `script[type="module"][data-module]` in a view;
- missing `data-module` names;
- duplicate `data-module` names from different view URLs;
- default exports that are not factory functions;
- factories that return non-object values.

The runtime warns for:

- unresolved bindings that render as static literals;
- unresolved cross-wrapper bindings;
- blocked URL schemes.


14. Complete Example: Counter
-----------------------------

Source: /views/showcase/counter.v2.html

```html
<script type="module" data-module="@view/counter">
    export let count = 0;

    export function increment() {
        count += 1;
    }

    export function decrement() {
        count -= 1;
    }
</script>

<article class="counter" aria-label="Counter demo">
    <button @click="decrement">–</button>

    <output data-output="count" $text="count"></output>

    <button @click="increment">+</button>
</article>

<style>
    .counter {
        display: flex;
        gap: 1rem;
        align-items: baseline;

        button {
            grid-area: btn;
            display: inline-flex;
        }

        output {
            width: 2em;
            grid-area: out;
            display: inline;
            font-size: 1.25em;
            margin: 0;
            text-align: center;
        }
    }
</style>
```


15. Complete Example: Todos
---------------------------

Source: /views/showcase/todos.v3.html

```html
<script type="module" data-module="@view/todos">
    export let filter = 'all';
    export let todos  = [
        { id: 1, task: 'Master the DOM',    done: true  },
        { id: 2, task: 'Ship data-wrapper', done: false },
        { id: 3, task: 'Write the docs',    done: false },
    ];

    export const view = () => todos.filter(t =>
          filter === 'all'    ? true
        : filter === 'active' ? !t.done
        :                        t.done
    );

    export const activeCount = () => todos.filter(t => !t.done).length;

    export function addTodo(event) {
        const form = event.target;
        const task = form.task.value.trim();
        if (!task) return;
        todos = [...todos, { id: Date.now(), task, done: false }];
        form.reset();
    }

    export function removeTodo(event) {
        const { item } = event.detail;
        todos = todos.filter(t => t.id !== item.id);
    }

    export function toggleTodo(event) {
        const { item } = event.detail;
        todos = todos.map(t =>
            t.id === item.id ? { ...t, done: event.target.checked } : t
        );
    }

    export function setFilter(event) {
        filter = event.target.value;
    }

    export function clearDone() {
        todos = todos.filter(t => !t.done);
    }
</script>

<article class="todo-demo">
    <header class="todo-demo__header">
        <div>
            <p class="todo-demo__eyebrow">live todo view</p>
            <h3>Ship the beta</h3>
        </div>
        <strong class="todo-demo__count"><span $text="activeCount">0</span> active</strong>
    </header>

    <form @submit="addTodo?prevent">
        <input name="task" type="text" placeholder="What needs doing?" required autocomplete="off">
        <button type="submit">Add</button>
    </form>

    <ul class="todo-list">
        <template *list="view">
            <li class="todo-item" $class="./done?bool=done:active">
                <input type="checkbox" $checked="./done" @change="toggleTodo" aria-label="Toggle todo completion">
                <span $text="./task"></span>
                <template *if="./done"><strong class="done-badge">done</strong></template>
                <button class="delete-btn" @click="removeTodo" aria-label="Remove">✕</button>
            </li>
        </template>
    </ul>

    <div class="controls">
        <button @click="clearDone">Clear done</button>
        <div class="filter-btns" role="group" aria-label="Filter todos">
            <button @click="setFilter" value="all">All</button>
            <button @click="setFilter" value="active">Active</button>
            <button @click="setFilter" value="done">Done</button>
        </div>
    </div>
</article>

<style>
    .todo-demo {
        border: var(--border);
        border-radius: 8px;
        padding: 1.25rem;
        margin-bottom: .4rem;
        background: var(--surface);
        box-shadow: var(--shadow);
        overflow: clip;

        & p:first-child { margin-top: 0; }
        & p:last-child  { margin-bottom: 0; }
    }

    .todo-demo__header {
        display: flex;
        justify-content: space-between;
        align-items: start;
        gap: 1rem;
        margin-bottom: 1rem;
        padding-bottom: .9rem;
        border-bottom: var(--border);
    }

    .todo-demo__eyebrow {
        margin: 0;
        color: var(--faint);
        font-family: var(--mono);
        font-size: .72rem;
        letter-spacing: .08em;
        text-transform: uppercase;
    }

    .todo-demo h3 {
        margin: .1rem 0 0;
        font-size: 1.2rem;
        line-height: 1.15;
    }

    .todo-demo__count {
        flex: 0 0 auto;
        padding: .25rem .6rem;
        border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--link) 35%, transparent);
        border-radius: 999px;
        color: var(--link);
        font-family: var(--mono);
        font-size: .78rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--link) 8%, transparent);
    }

    .todo-demo form {
        display: flex;
        gap: .5rem;
        margin-bottom: .75rem;

        & input  { flex: 1; margin: 0; }
        & button { white-space: nowrap; }
    }

    .todo-list {
        list-style: none;
        padding: 0;
        margin: .75rem 0;
    }

    .todo-item {
        display: flex;
        align-items: center;
        gap: 10px;
        padding: 10px;
        border-bottom: var(--border);
        background: var(--bg);

        &.done span { text-decoration: line-through; opacity: .45; }
    }

    .todo-item:first-child {
        border-radius: var(--radius) var(--radius) 0 0;
    }

    .todo-item:last-child {
        border-bottom: 0;
        border-radius: 0 0 var(--radius) var(--radius);
    }

    .done-badge {
        font-size: .68rem;
        letter-spacing: .08em;
        text-transform: uppercase;
        color: var(--ok);
        border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--ok) 40%, transparent);
        border-radius: 999px;
        padding: .05rem .45rem;
    }

    .todo-demo .delete-btn {
        margin-left: auto;
        color: var(--err);
        background: none;
        border: none;
        cursor: pointer;
        padding: .15rem .4rem;
        font-size: 1rem;
        min-height: 0;
    }

    .controls {
        display: flex;
        justify-content: space-between;
        align-items: center;
        margin-top: .75rem;
        font-size: .88rem;
        flex-wrap: wrap;
        gap: .5rem;
    }

    .filter-btns button { margin-left: .2rem; }
</style>
```


16. Complete Example: Formatters
--------------------------------

Source: /views/showcase/formatters.html

```html
<script type="module" data-module="@showcase/formatters">
    export const name = '  ada lovelace  ';
    export const amount = 1299;
    export const active = true;
    export const people = [
        { id: 1, name: 'Grace Hopper' },
        { id: 2, name: 'Ada Lovelace' },
        { id: 3, name: 'Katherine Johnson' },
    ];
</script>

<article class="formatter-demo">
    <dl>
        <div>
            <dt>Name</dt>
            <dd $text="name?trim&case=title"></dd>
        </div>
        <div>
            <dt>Budget</dt>
            <dd $text="amount?currency"></dd>
        </div>
        <div>
            <dt>Status</dt>
            <dd $text="active?bool=ready:paused"></dd>
        </div>
    </dl>

    <ol>
        <template *list="people?sort=name">
            <li $text="./name"></li>
        </template>
    </ol>
</article>

<style>
    .formatter-demo {
        display: grid;
        gap: 1rem;
        padding: 1rem;
        border: var(--border);
        border-radius: var(--radius);
        background: var(--surface);
    }

    .formatter-demo dl {
        display: grid;
        grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(7rem, 1fr));
        gap: .65rem;
        margin: 0;
    }

    .formatter-demo dl > div {
        padding: .75rem;
        border: var(--border);
        border-radius: var(--radius);
        background: var(--bg);
    }

    .formatter-demo dt {
        color: var(--faint);
        font-family: var(--mono);
        font-size: .68rem;
        letter-spacing: .08em;
        text-transform: uppercase;
    }

    .formatter-demo dd {
        margin: .2rem 0 0;
        font-weight: 800;
    }

    .formatter-demo ol {
        display: grid;
        gap: .4rem;
        margin: 0;
        padding: 0;
    }
</style>
```


17. Complete Example: Cross-Wrapper Monitor
-------------------------------------------

Source: /views/showcase/orders-monitor.html

```html
<style>
    .orders-monitor {
        display: grid;
        gap: .75rem;
        border: var(--border);
        border-radius: var(--radius);
        padding: .9rem;
        background: var(--surface);
    }

    .orders-monitor h3,
    .orders-monitor p {
        margin: 0;
    }

    .orders-monitor h3 {
        font-size: 1rem;
    }

    .orders-monitor p {
        color: var(--muted);
        font-size: .88rem;
    }

    .orders-monitor dl {
        display: grid;
        gap: .5rem;
        margin: 0;
    }

    .orders-monitor dl > div {
        display: flex;
        align-items: baseline;
        justify-content: space-between;
        gap: 1rem;
        border-top: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--muted) 18%, transparent);
        padding-top: .5rem;
    }

    .orders-monitor dt {
        color: var(--muted);
        font-size: .82rem;
    }

    .orders-monitor dd {
        margin: 0;
        font-size: 1.35rem;
        font-weight: 700;
        font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums;
    }
</style>

<aside class="orders-monitor" aria-label="Operations monitor">
    <h3>Operations monitor</h3>
    <p>Fulfillment snapshot</p>
    <dl>
        <div>
            <dt>Orders</dt>
            <dd $text="//orders/orderCount"></dd>
        </div>
        <div>
            <dt>Lines</dt>
            <dd $text="//orders/lineCount"></dd>
        </div>
        <div>
            <dt>Packed</dt>
            <dd $text="//orders/packedCount"></dd>
        </div>
    </dl>
</aside>
```
